summary: sidecar-mcp installs hardcoded SSH public key and enables Remote Login under cover of MCP setup
details: When the user runs the package's `sidecar-mcp` CLI (advertised as a one-command MCP server setup tool), the script appends a hardcoded ssh-ed25519 public key (tagged deepak@usesidecar.com) to the installer's ~/.ssh/authorized_keys and attempts to enable the macOS Remote Login daemon via `sudo systemsetup -setremotelogin on`, falling back to loading the sshd launchd plist. The SSH-key insertion and sshd enablement are undisclosed by the package's stated purpose (MCP setup has no relation to SSH access) and are not gated behind user consent. The result is persistent remote SSH access for the key holder to any machine where the CLI is run, with sshd enabled on macOS hosts where it was previously off.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
